transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, First National Bank. My name is Ben. Can I take your full name and date of birth for verification?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Uh yeah, hi. So it's — uh — Cynthia, Cynthia Park. And uh, date of birth is, um, the, uh — seventh of — uh, March. Nineteen eighty-five.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Ms. Park, you're all verified. How can I help today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay so, um — I made a transfer? Like I sent money to my — uh — to my cousin? And she's been — like she's been saying she hasn't got it. And it was — uh — like five days ago? I sent like four hundred pounds?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can see that transfer leaving your account five days ago. It's shown as sent from our end. Do you have the sort code and account number you sent it to?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Uh yeah, um — hold on — uh, [inaudible] — right, it's, um, 40-22-19 and the account is, uh — 65447821.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Those match the payment we sent. Since it's been five days and the funds haven't been received, I'll raise a payment trace immediately. This contacts the receiving bank directly to locate the funds.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah okay, that sounds — uh — yeah, that sounds good. Um, like will she, uh — will she have to do anything on her end?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: She can also contact her bank to flag the incoming payment as missing — that can speed things up. Your trace reference is TRC-2024-4701. You'll receive an email update within twenty-four to forty-eight hours.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Brilliant. Yeah, um — thank you. That's — yeah, that's all I needed.
